mission.title: flux: HelmRelease Values from targetPath array
mission.description: HelmRelease Values from targetPath array. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux deploy symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: HelmRelease Values from targetPath array

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Describe the bug

Hello, I cannot seem to be able to use the `targetFrom` field to access an existing array within the `valuesFrom` object in a helm release.

### Steps to reproduce

In the main v

steps.title: Apply the fix for HelmRelease Values from targetPath array
steps.description:
Fix: https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/2330

When using Helm with both a values file and --set, the set will always have precedence over the values.yaml. This allows specifying the normal config inside a yaml file and specifying a secret outside that yaml via --set.

When using flux, this norma
```yaml
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2beta1
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: release-name
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  values:
    confd:
      file.yaml:
        init_config:
          service: kafka-consumers
        instances:
          - kafka_connect_str: xxx:9092
            monitor_unlisted_consumer_groups: true
```

steps.title: Confirm HelmRelease Values from targetPath array is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Fix: https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/2330

When using Helm with both a values file and --set, the set will always have precedence over the values.yaml. This allows specifying the normal config inside a yaml file and specifying a secret outside that yaml via --set.

When using flux, this normally works too (using inline values to specify most of the config and a reference with targetPath to . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2beta1
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: release-name
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  values:
    confd:
      file.yaml:
        init_config:
          service: kafka-consumers
        instances:
          - kafka_connect_str: xxx:9092
            monitor_unlisted_consumer_groups: true

valuesFrom:
    - kind: Secret
      name: secrets
      valuesKey: username
      targetPath: confd.file\.yaml.instances[0].sasl_plain_username
    - kind: Secret
      name: secrets
      valuesKey: password
      targetPath: confd.file\.yaml.instances[0].sasl_plain_password

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
